summary.intent: Implement auto-cleanup of orphaned cron sessions and sandbox containers in the moltis project, based on GitHub discussion #538.
summary.outcome: All 5 phases fully implemented across 15 files: data model changes, session_key threading, auto-prune container logic, periodic session retention pruning, and tests. 644 tests pass, clippy clean, commit created.

repo: CronStore has 3 implementations: SQLite (store_sqlite.rs), file (store_file.rs), and in-memory (store_memory.rs) — all must be kept in sync when the trait changes.
Config for cron lives in crates/config/src/schema.rs (CronConfig struct), with schema map registration in validate.rs and template documentation in template.rs.
The gateway's on_agent_turn closure cannot access config via state.inner — config must be captured as a local variable before the closure is created.
cron_store is moved into CronService::with_events_queue, so it must be cloned (Arc clone) before that call if needed elsewhere.
cleanup_session exists on SandboxRouter for container cleanup after cron runs.
GPG commit signing requires YubiKey tap — commits will fail/hang without it.
CronSandboxConfig is constructed in multiple crates: cron/types.rs, gateway/methods/services.rs, httpd/src/server.rs, httpd/tests/heartbeat_cron.rs — all need updates when fields are added.
default_true helper function already existed at schema.rs:1291 — adding a duplicate causes a compile error.
Clippy requires collapsible if-let patterns to be merged; this affected store_file.rs, store_memory.rs, and gateway/server.rs.

code.finding: CronConfig struct location; new fields session_retention_days and auto_prune_cron_containers added here
default_true helper function lives here — do not add duplicates earlier in the file
on_agent_turn closure: Phase 2 (thread session_key) and Phase 3 (auto-prune container) implemented here; auto_prune_cron_containers must be captured before closure
Periodic pruning background task (Phase 4) spawned here after startup GC block
CronStore trait — added prune_runs_before method that all 3 implementations must satisfy

workflow: Beads issue tracking used with dependency chain notation (wpf → iw1 → {375, 4gb} → 6j1) to model phase dependencies.
cargo check used per-crate before running full workspace check to surface errors faster.
clippy run scoped to modified crates (--package flags) to avoid pre-existing CUDA/cmake build failures on macOS.
cargo fmt run before final clippy pass to avoid formatting-related lint noise.

friction: GPG/YubiKey signing required physical tap for commit — caused the commit to stall and require a retry.
Workspace-wide clippy fails due to pre-existing CUDA crate build issues on macOS; had to scope clippy to modified crates only.
default_true duplicate compile error from adding a helper that already existed 300+ lines later in the same file.
CronSandboxConfig struct additions required hunting down construction sites across 4 different crates.
config access pattern in on_agent_turn closure was non-obvious — state.inner doesn't have config, requiring capture of the value before the closure.
open_items: Phase 5 integration tests for session pruning and container auto-prune were not explicitly implemented — only unit/store tests were added.
The periodic pruning task queries sessions by cron: prefix and checks active job names — this logic should be validated against edge cases (renamed jobs, job deletion mid-prune).
Named sessions are excluded from retention pruning by design, but there's no cleanup path for named sessions belonging to deleted cron jobs.
Cron docs update (mentioned in Phase 5 plan) was not explicitly done beyond the config template comments.
